target_statement: Determine the optimal constant μ such that lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π) = μ under RH

propositions.statement: RH: All non-trivial zeros of ζ(s) lie on Re(s) = 1/2, so ρ_n = 1/2 + iγ_n with γ_n real and increasing
Explicit formula: ψ(x) = x - Σ_ρ (x^ρ / ρ) + O(1) where sum is over non-trivial zeros
Montgomery pair correlation conjecture: for φ with Fourier transform compactly supported in (-1,1), lim_{T→∞} (1/N(T)) Σ_{0<γ,γ'≤T} φ((γ-γ')/(2π/log T)) = ∫_{-∞}^{∞} φ(x)(1 - (sin πx / πx)²) dx
Current best rigorous result: under RH, lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π) < 0.50895
Under RH, variance bound: V(T) = ∫_T^{2T} |ψ(e^t) - e^t|² dt = O(T² log T)
Density of zeros: N(T) ~ (T log T)/(2π) as T → ∞
Define gap-forcing functional F_T(δ) = sup_{T ≤ t ≤ 2T} |Σ_{Δγ_n < δ, γ_n ~ t} (e^{t/2} cos(t log t))/γ_n| where sum is over zeros with normalized gap less than δ
Define M_T(δ) = #{n : T ≤ γ_n ≤ 2T and (γ_{n+1} - γ_n)·log(γ_n)/(2π) < δ} as count of normalized gaps less than δ in window [T, 2T]
Variance contribution from abnormally close zeros satisfies: V_{small}(T, δ) ≥ M_T(δ) · T/(log T)² · δ² for small δ
From variance bound V(T) = O(T² log T) and V_{small} contribution, derive constraint: M_T(δ) ≲ T(log T)³/δ² for any fixed δ > 0
Under Montgomery pair correlation conjecture, the probability that a normalized gap is less than s is P(d_n < s) = ∫_0^s S(u) du = 1 - exp(-πs²/4) - s√(π/2) exp(-πs²/4)
If lim inf (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π) = 0, then for any ε > 0, there exist arbitrarily large T such that M_T(ε) ≥ c·N(T) for some constant c > 0 depending on the GUE distribution
The configuration in P13 contradicts the constraint M_T(ε) ≲ T(log T)³/ε² from P11 when ε is sufficiently small, forcing lim inf > 0
Define μ_candidate = sup{δ > 0 : lim inf_{T→∞} M_T(δ)/N(T) = 0} as a candidate for the optimal constant
Balance condition for determining μ: the value satisfies ∫_0^{μ} s·S(s) ds · (mean density factor) = O(1) where S is GUE spacing
Under the balance condition P16, estimate μ ≥ 0.01
Explicit computation formula: μ = inf{s > 0 : ∫_0^s u·S(u) du · √(log T)/s ≫ 1} where the condition must hold for all large T

Under RH and Montgomery's pair correlation conjecture, there exists an explicit constant μ > 0 such that lim inf_{n→∞} (γ_{n+1} - γ_n) log(γ_n) / (2π) = μ, and this constant can be determined via variance-gap analysis

chain.statement: Establish seeds: RH places zeros on critical line (P1), explicit formula relates ψ to zeros (P2), Montgomery conjecture gives pair correlation (P3), known bounds and density (P4, P6, P7), GUE spacing distribution (P5)

Introduce auxiliary objects: gap-forcing functional F_T(δ) measuring contribution of close zeros to explicit formula oscillations, and gap counter M_T(δ) counting normalized gaps less than δ in [T, 2T]
Claim variance decomposition: contribution from close zeros V_small(T,δ) is bounded below by M_T(δ) · T/(log T)² · δ², connecting gap count to variance
Derive constraint on gap count: combining variance bound V(T) = O(T² log T) with the variance decomposition P10 implies M_T(δ) ≲ T(log T)³/δ²
Extract gap probability from Montgomery: assuming pair correlation follows GUE, integrate spacing distribution S(s) to get cumulative probability P(d_n < s) = 1 - exp(-πs²/4) - s√(π/2) exp(-πs²/4)
Attempt contradiction argument: if μ = 0, claim that GUE statistics imply M_T(ε) ≥ c·N(T) for small ε, which would contradict the constraint M_T(ε) ≲ T(log T)³/ε²
Propose supremum definition: define μ_candidate = sup{δ : lim inf M_T(δ)/N(T) = 0} and claim this equals the actual lim inf of normalized gaps
Propose balance condition and computation formula: suggest μ can be computed via balance ∫_0^μ s·S(s) ds ~ O(1) or via inf{s : ∫_0^s u·S(u) du · √(log T)/s ≫ 1}

Conclude target: under RH and Montgomery, claim that μ exists as a positive constant and can be determined, though the complete argument has unresolved gaps in the contradiction logic and computation formulas

summary: The route attempts to determine the optimal constant μ for normalized Riemann zero gaps under RH by connecting variance bounds on ψ(x) oscillations to gap count constraints, combined with Montgomery's pair correlation conjecture predicting GUE gap statistics. The approach introduces testable variance inequalities (grade C: P10, P11, P12) but contains significant unresolved gaps in the contradiction logic (grade D: P13, P14) and ad-hoc computation formulas (grade D: P15-P18).
